title: Use a Calendar
summary: Keep track of events and deadlines so nothing catches you off guard.
learnerPromise: Keep track of events and deadlines so nothing catches you off guard.
whyItMatters: A calendar turns chaos into order. It reduces stress and helps you plan your time with more confidence.
realLifeUses: Track assignment due dates for school.
Plan around upcoming events and deadlines.
Coordinate a time that works for a group.
youWillLearn: Add events with dates and times.
miniChallenge: Add one real event to your calendar and set a reminder for it.
steps: Choose a calendar: paper, phone app, or computer.
Add every event you know about for the next week.
Set a reminder one day before each important event.
Pick a time each day to check your calendar.
Update it immediately when you learn about a new event.
completionCriteria: Your calendar has at least five events entered.
You check it at least once per day for three days.
commonProblems: Not writing down events right away.
Setting too many reminders and ignoring them all.
tips: Make checking your calendar part of your morning routine.
Set only important reminders so you pay attention to them.
Use different colors for school, work, and personal events.
